name: context-update
description: Detect and propose edits when reusable-context files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, plans, personal instructions) drift from decisions made in the current conversation.
skillInstructions:
Kimi Code tool mapping for the context-update skill:

- When the skill says to ask the user to disambiguate (e.g. Project vs Personal context blocks on no-fs runtimes) or to confirm an Apply? decision, call Kimi Code's `AskUserQuestion` tool with 1 question and 2-4 concrete options. Put the recommended option (usually the safest non-destructive choice) first with `(Recommended)`.
- When the skill refers to `TodoWrite`, use Kimi Code's `TodoList` tool.
- When the skill refers to the `Skill` tool (e.g. "invoke the context-update skill"), use Kimi Code's native `Skill` tool.
- When the skill asks to dispatch a subagent (rare for context-update; only used for parallel file inspection), call `Agent` with `subagent_type: "explore"` for read-only scans and `subagent_type: "coder"` only if a follow-up edit subagent is required. Never pass `general-purpose`.
- Use Kimi Code's `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Bash`, `Grep`, `Glob`, `FetchURL`, `WebSearch`, and MCP tools by their actual exposed names. For "read a file fresh" use `Read`; for "apply the approved diff" use `Edit`; for "create the new `.contextupdate.toml`" use `Write`.
- For the Step 7 config write, re-read `.contextupdate.toml` with `Read` before applying any `Edit` — the iron law against blind writes applies to the config file too.
- Never modify a watched context file without an explicit per-file `y` / `apply N` / `apply all` reply from the user, regardless of which tool you would otherwise use.

interface.shortDescription: Detect drift between conversation decisions and reusable-context files
interface.longDescription: Use Context Update at session wrap-up, after a preference reversal, or whenever a conversation has produced decisions that may contradict statements written in CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, implementation plans, or personal instructions. The skill enumerates watched files, extracts conversation decisions, surfaces contradictions and stale statements, and proposes per-file edits — never auto-applying.
